Subsection.Text: In addition to the broadcasting time to be made available under section 335, and within the period referred to in that section, every broadcaster shall, subject to the regulations made under the Broadcasting Act and to the conditions imposed on it under section 9.1 of that Act, make available, for purchase by every eligible party entitled to broadcasting time under this section, broadcasting time in the amount determined under this section for the eligible party for the transmission of political announcements and other programming produced by or on behalf of the eligible party during prime time on that broadcasterâs facilities.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 345(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In the period beginning on the issue of the writs for a general election and ending at midnight on the day before polling day at that election, every network operator shall, subject to the regulations made under the Broadcasting Act and to the conditions imposed on it under section 9.1 of that Act, make available, at no cost, to the registered parties and eligible parties referred to in subsection (2), for the transmission of political announcements and other programming produced by or on behalf of those parties, broadcasting time as determined under that subsection if the network formed and operated by the network operator

Section.Text: Paragraph 42(1)(b) of the Accessible Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions imposed on the regulated entity under section 9.1 of the Broadcasting Act that relate to the identification and removal of barriers and the prevention of new barriers;

Section.Text: Paragraph 118(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a condition imposed under section 9.1 of the Broadcasting Act;

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and sections 43 to 46.

Definition.Text: new Actâmeans the Broadcasting Act as it reads as of the royal assent day.â(nouvelle loi)
old Actâmeans the Broadcasting Act as it read immediately before the royal assent day.â(ancienne loi)
royal assent dayâmeans the day on which this Act receives royal assent.â(date de sanction)

Subsection.Text: Unless otherwise provided, words and expressions used in sections 43 to 46 have the same meanings as in the Broadcasting Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Conditions and requirements â deemed order

Subsection.Text: Each of the following is deemed to be a condition imposed under an order, made under section 9.1 of the new Act, that applies only with respect to a particular licensee:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a condition of their licence imposed under section 9 of the old Act that, as of the royal assent day, could not be made the subject of an order under subsection 11.1(2) of the new Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement imposed on the licensee under any of paragraphs 9(1)(f) to (h) of the old Act.

Subsection.Text: Any regulation made under paragraph 10(1)(a) or 10(1)(i) of the old Act is deemed to be an order made under section 9.1 of the new Act.
The following are deemed to be regulations made under subsection 11.1(1) of the new Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any terms and conditions imposed under an order made under subsection 9(4) of the old Act that, as of the royal assent day, could be the subject of such regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any regulations made under subsection 10(1) of the old Act that, as of the royal assent day, could be made under subsection 11.1(1) of the new Act.

Subsection.Text: Any condition of a licenseeâs licence that, as of the royal assent day, could be made the subject of an order under subsection 11.1(2) of the new Act is deemed to be a provision of such an order that applies only with respect to the licensee.
Section 28 of the old Act continues to apply with respect to any decision of the Commission to issue, amend or renew a licence that is made before the royal assent day.

A person is not permitted to make a petition â and the Governor in Council is not permitted to make an order â under subsection 28(1) of the new Act with respect to a decision to renew a licence made by the Commission during the transition period if the Commission specifies, in renewing that licence, that it is an interim licence and if its term is for no more than one year.
In subsection (2), transition period means the period beginning on the royal assent day and ending on the second anniversary of that day.
The expenditures described in subsection (2) are deemed to have been validly required by the Commission under the old Act.
Subsection (1) applies with respect to the expenditures â including, for greater certainty, the contributions â that were made by a broadcasting undertaking before the royal assent day under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a condition of a licence issued under the old Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a term or condition of an order made under subsection 9(4) of that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: regulations made under section 10 of that Act.

Section.Text: Subsections 34(2) to (4) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
